AI agents change the math of infrastructure.
“One app serves many users” → millions of agents, each needing compute.
Anni Wang explains why scale looks very different now.

.@Claynosaurz Co-Founder @Cabanimation on why they chose Solana

"It felt like a decentralized Silicon Valley. Clusters of team-building startups, then clusters of devs branching out and building other startups. Everyone knew everyone."

"There was beautiful cross-pollination between the tech startups being built on Solana and the NFT and art communities. It had culture."

"You need tech, you need art, and you need NFTs, which I always felt were the nightclubs in a city."

"We're growing an entertainment brand targeted for mass consumption. It's very hard to do that if there's a lot of friction."

"Solana felt right for us because we wanted to do experiences. We wanted to entertain people. Gamified collectible loops. Exploring what it means to put assets inside games onchain."
@phil_hellmuth @rajgokal ♠️♥️♣️♦️

current agentic commerce protocols glossary:

������ x402 - @CoinbaseDev's HTTP-native payment protocol built on the 402 status code, letting agents pay for resources inline, without wallets or human checkouts

������ A2A (agent-to-agent) - @googlecloud's open protocol for agents to discover, communicate with, and delegate tasks to other agents across different frameworks

������ ADK (agent development kit) - google's open-source toolkit for building and orchestrating multi-agent systems, designed to work natively with A2A

������ ERC-8004 - an emerging ethereum standard for defining agent spending permissions onchain, giving humans a way to set limits on what agents can authorize

������️ MPP (machine payment protocol) - a protocol for machine-to-machine payments focused on low-latency, automated settlement between software agents

������️ ACP (agent communication protocol) - @Cisco's framework for standardizing how agents exchange context, state, and instructions across enterprise environments

������ AP2 (agent payment protocol) - a payment layer designed specifically for agent workflows, handling authorization, routing, and settlement between autonomous systems

drop a comment if anything is missing ������

Block laid off 4,000 people. Dorsey cited "the growing capability of AI tools to perform a wider range of tasks."

Those tasks won't have vanished. They're migrated to agents. 

Agents doing contract work need the same infrastructure humans had: payment, identity, proof of completion, dispute resolution.

Best X agent

Curate all the best in-depth review sites by category -- subreddits, Wirecutter, Outdoor Gear Lab, America’s Test Kitchen, Serious Eats, etc -- and then the agent knows your preferences, e.g. always pick the recommended / upgrade / budget option, buy on direct website vs. Amazon.

Returns the result in a nicely formatted webpage that focuses on what's most important to you when making a purchase.

Give 100% of affiliate link revenue to the review site, charge a small fee for the agent query.
